privacy-and-surveillance.summary: The Senate's leading privacy champion, fiercely opposes warrantless surveillance and the PATRIOT Act.

trade-and-tariffs.summary: Opposes tariffs as a hidden tax on consumers, supports free trade consistent with libertarian principles.

economy-and-jobs.summary: Supports tax cuts and pro-business policies as demonstrated by his championing of the 2017 tax law.

healthcare-and-medicare.summary: Led efforts to repeal the ACA and supports market-based healthcare reforms.

immigration-and-border-security.summary: Supports border security while occasionally working across the aisle on immigration compromise.

education-and-student-debt.summary: Opposes student debt cancellation and supports local control of education.

national-defense-and-military.summary: One of the Senate's strongest defense hawks, consistently supporting military spending increases.

climate-and-environment.summary: Opposes climate regulations, particularly those affecting Kentucky's coal industry.

criminal-justice-reform.summary: Generally favors tough-on-crime policies but did not block the First Step Act.

foreign-policy-and-diplomacy.summary: Strong internationalist who champions NATO, Ukraine aid, and American global leadership.

technology-and-ai-regulation.summary: Generally opposes broad tech regulation but supports targeted national security measures.

housing-and-affordability.summary: Prefers market solutions and opposes large federal housing programs.

reproductive-rights.summary: Pro-life and worked to confirm Supreme Court justices who overturned Roe v. Wade.

lgbtq-rights.summary: Generally opposes federal LGBTQ legislation though voted for the Respect for Marriage Act.

drug-policy.summary: Opposes marijuana legalization and supports hemp production for Kentucky farmers.

voting-rights.summary: Blocked federal voting rights legislation, arguing for state control of elections.

labor-and-unions.summary: Opposes expanding union power and the PRO Act.
